1.title: A prophet's grandfather
1.text: Zechariah is introduced as 'the son of Berechiah, the son of Iddo' — a rare case of a prophet whose grandfather is named. Iddo was a priest, so Zechariah was of a priestly line.

1.question: What repeated appeal does the Lord make in Zechariah 1:3?
options: 'Turn ye unto me, and I will turn unto you'
'Fear not, for I am with thee'

1.question: In the first night vision, on what did the man among the myrtle trees ride (Zechariah 1:8)?

1.template: my house shall be built in it

1.question: What did the four carpenters come to do to the four horns (Zechariah 1:21)?
options: Fray them and cast them out

2.title: A city without walls
2.text: The man goes to measure Jerusalem, yet the vision promises it will be 'inhabited as towns without walls' — so vast and secure that no wall could contain it, guarded instead by God himself.

2.template: will be unto her a wall of {0} round about

2.question: In Zechariah 2:8, what does the Lord call the one who touches his people?
How does Zechariah 2:13 say all flesh should respond before the Lord?

3.question: In Zechariah 3:4, what does the angel do with Joshua's filthy garments?
options: Takes them away and clothes him with change of raiment

3.title: The servant called the Branch
3.text: Verse 8 announces God's servant 'the Branch' — a messianic title Zechariah returns to in 6:12. The image is of a shoot sprouting from David's fallen line, pointing to the Messiah.

3.question: In Zechariah 3:10, under what two trees will each man invite his neighbour?
options: The vine and the fig tree

4.title: A lampstand of pure gold
4.text: The candlestick 'all of gold' with seven lamps recalls the menorah of the tabernacle. Here it symbolizes God's presence and Spirit lighting the rebuilt temple.

4.question: When the angel asks if Zechariah knows what the vision means, what does he answer (Zechariah 4:5)?

4.template: who hath despised the day of small {0}

4.question: Who are the two olive trees said to be in Zechariah 4:14?
options: The two anointed ones that stand by the Lord

5.title: A flying scroll
5.text: The sixth vision is a huge flying scroll, twenty cubits long — about the size of the tabernacle's holy place. It carries a curse against thieves and those who swear falsely.

5.question: According to Zechariah 5:4, the curse enters the house of the thief and of whom?
options: Him that sweareth falsely by God's name

pairs.right: A curse over the whole earth

6.question: What does the angel say the four chariots are in Zechariah 6:5?
options: The four kings of the east
The four spirits of the heavens

6.question: In Zechariah 6:12, what is the name of the man who shall build the temple of the Lord?

6.title: King and priest in one
6.text: Verse 13 says the Branch 'shall be a priest upon his throne,' uniting king and priest — offices normally kept apart in Israel. It looks forward to the Messiah, who fills both roles.

7.title: Fasting for a fallen city
7.text: The people ask whether to keep weeping and fasting in the fifth month — the month Jerusalem and its temple had been burned by Babylon decades earlier. The Lord questions whether such fasts were ever for him.

7.question: In Zechariah 7:5-6, the Lord asks whether the people fasted and ate for whom?
Whom does Zechariah 7:10 command the people not to oppress?
options: The widow, the fatherless, the stranger, and the poor

8.template: the streets of the city shall be full of boys and {0}

8.question: According to Zechariah 8:3, what will Jerusalem be called when the Lord returns?
In Zechariah 8:13, the Lord says that as Judah and Israel were a curse among the nations, so he will make them a what?
According to Zechariah 8:19, the fasts shall become joy, gladness, and what for the house of Judah?
In Zechariah 8:23, ten men take hold of the skirt of a Jew, saying they will go with him because they have heard what?

8.title: Old age as a blessing
8.text: The vision of old men and women sitting in the streets, each with a staff 'for very age,' was itself a promise of peace: only a city safe from war and famine could grow old.

9.question: In Zechariah 9:3-4, Tyre heaped up silver and gold, yet the Lord says she shall be devoured with what?
In Zechariah 9:8, the Lord says he will encamp about his house so that no what shall pass through them any more?

9.template: he shall speak {0} unto the heathen

9.question: In Zechariah 9:9, how does the King come to the daughter of Zion?
options: Lowly, and riding upon an ass

9.title: The King on a donkey
9.text: 'Thy King cometh... lowly, and riding upon an ass' (v. 9) was fulfilled when Jesus rode into Jerusalem on Palm Sunday — a king of peace, not on a war horse.

10.title: No shepherd, no rain
10.text: The chapter opens by contrasting idols and diviners — who 'comfort in vain' — with the Lord, who gives real rain. Without the true shepherd, the people wandered 'as a flock' with no one to guide them.

10.question: In Zechariah 10:6-7, whose houses does the Lord promise to strengthen and save?

10.template: they shall walk up and down in his {0}

11.question: In Zechariah 11:4-5, the flock of slaughter is sold by owners who say what?
options: 'Blessed be the Lord; for I am rich'

11.question: What were the names of the two staves the shepherd took in Zechariah 11:7?

11.template: So they weighed for my price thirty pieces of {0}

11.question: Zechariah 11:17 pronounces woe on what kind of shepherd?
options: The idol shepherd that leaveth the flock

11.title: Thirty pieces of silver
11.text: The shepherd's wage — thirty pieces of silver, thrown to the potter in the house of the Lord (vv. 12-13) — is echoed in the New Testament as the price paid to Judas for betraying Jesus.

12.template: The inhabitants of Jerusalem shall be my {0}

12.question: In Zechariah 12:10, the Lord pours out the spirit of grace, and the people look upon whom?
According to Zechariah 12:12, how shall the land mourn?

12.title: Whom they have pierced
12.text: 'They shall look upon me whom they have pierced, and they shall mourn' (v. 10) is applied in the Gospel of John to the crucifixion of Jesus, when a soldier pierced his side.

13.title: A fountain for cleansing
13.text: The chapter opens with 'a fountain opened... for sin and for uncleanness' (v. 1). Christian readers have long seen in it a picture of the cleansing offered through the Messiah.

13.question: In Zechariah 13:5, how does the man deny being a prophet?
options: 'I am no prophet, I am an husbandman'

13.question: In Zechariah 13:9, how does the Lord refine the third part that is left?
options: As silver is refined, and tried as gold

14.question: In Zechariah 14:4, upon what mountain shall the Lord's feet stand in that day?
According to Zechariah 14:7, what strange thing will happen at evening time in that day?

14.template: Their flesh shall consume away while they stand upon their {0}

14.question: In Zechariah 14:9, over what shall the Lord be king in that day?

14.title: Holiness even on the horses' bells
14.text: Verse 20 says 'Holiness unto the Lord' shall be inscribed even on the bells of the horses — a picture of a day when the ordinary and everyday become as sacred as the temple vessels.

14.question: In Zechariah 14:21, what shall no longer be in the house of the Lord of hosts?
